summary: Large fighter-collision decompilation PR centered on src/melee/ft/ftcoll.c. It replaced many NOT_IMPLEMENTED stubs, improved several near-matching routines, corrected prototypes and struct fields needed by collision/damage-log logic, and raised decomp.dev matched code by +1688 bytes. The bot reported 8 new full function matches in main/melee/ft/ftcoll, 13 unmatched-item improvements, and one small .sdata regression.
what_changed: Added a substantial amount of real implementation code in src/melee/ft/ftcoll.c, replacing numerous NOT_IMPLEMENTED stubs in the ftColl_80077688 through ftColl_8007BE3C region.

Implemented item-vs-fighter collision paths such as ftColl_80077688, ftColl_80077970, ftColl_80077C60, and the large ftColl_8007925C item collision scanner, including shield, reflect, absorb, hit/hurt capsule, catch-element, and item hitbox interactions.

Implemented damage/knockback helper routines ftColl_80079AB0, ftColl_80079C70, and ftColl_80079EA8 using ftCommonData values, player attack/defense ratios, weight, stage multiplier, hit capsule fields, and percent/temp damage.
Implemented damage-log processing in ftColl_8007A06C, including choosing entries, computing knockback, spawning effects, and handling fighter/item sources based on DmgLogEntry.

Implemented smaller routines that became full matches according to the bot, including ftColl_80078538, ftColl_800787B4, ftColl_8007891C, ftColl_80078998, ftColl_8007AC9C, ftColl_8007B320, ftColl_8007B4E0, ftColl_8007BAC0, ftColl_8007BBCC, and ftColl_8007BC90.
Changed damage log indices dmg_log0_idx and dmg_log1_idx from size_t to int in ftcoll.c.

Replaced several OSReport plus HSD_ASSERT sequences with HSD_ASSERTREPORT at original line-number-style constants, including attack-power, damage-log, tip-log, illegal hit status, and hurt-capsule count assertions.
Corrected a damage-log write bug in ftColl_80076ED8 by writing the normal damage entry to dmg_log0[dmg_log0_idx] instead of dmg_log1[dmg_log1_idx].
Restructured ftColl_80076ED8 with PAD_STACK(32), explicit early goto retfalse flow, and HSD_ASSERTREPORT calls, improving its reported match from 94.35% to 97.73%.
Updated ftcoll.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS or imprecise parameter types to concrete signatures for ftColl_80077970, ftColl_80077C60, ftColl_80078538, ftColl_80078998, and ftColl_80079C70.
Added EntityKind_UNKNOWN to enum EntityKind in src/melee/ft/forward.h and used it as a default damage-log kind case in ftColl_8007BBCC.

Refined src/melee/ft/types.h layouts: ftData.x30 became a typed struct containing a count and ftHurtboxInit pointer; Fighter.x1670 element offset +0x24 became int x24 instead of padding; ftDynamics.x8 became struct ftData_x38*; DmgLogEntry.x20 became float.
smart_moves: Used the decomp.dev bot results to validate broad progress: 8 new full matches and 13 improved unmatched items in main/melee/ft/ftcoll, rather than only relying on local code shape.
Promoted unknown public interfaces to typed function signatures in ftcoll.h as implementation evidence became available, reducing future UNK_PARAMS churn.
Treated DmgLogEntry.x20 as float, matching how the diff stores temp damage and damage-log values, instead of keeping it as int.
Added a typed ftData_x30 wrapper for hurtbox initialization data, enabling ftColl_8007B320 and ftColl_8007B4E0 to read count/inits cleanly.
Matched assertion codegen by using HSD_ASSERTREPORT directly with numeric source-line constants, avoiding mismatching OSReport plus assert sequences.
Used PAD_STACK, #pragma dont_inline, #pragma push/pop, clang-format off, and explicit goto labels in hot collision routines where C structure needed to mirror original control flow.
Kept comments cautious where evidence was incomplete, for example the comment that ftColl_80078538's dmg parameter is probably a u32.
decomp_lessons: For ft collision work, struct/header corrections often unlock many function bodies at once; this PR's typed ftData.x30, ftDynamics.x8, and DmgLogEntry.x20 changes support multiple collision and damage-log implementations.
Damage-log arrays should distinguish normal damage and tip damage carefully: ftColl_80076ED8 writes normal entries to dmg_log0 and tip entries to dmg_log1.
When matching assert-heavy code, HSD_ASSERTREPORT may be the original form even if earlier code used OSReport followed by HSD_ASSERT.
Item/fighter collision scans may require preserving original early-exit and goto structure; simplifying the logic risks codegen drift in nested shield/reflect/absorb/catch paths.
Function signatures should be tightened as soon as callsites and implementations prove parameter types; this reduces casts and clarifies later matching work.
Small enum additions like EntityKind_UNKNOWN can be necessary to express original default cases and prevent misleading fallthrough behavior.
The bot can report a net matched-code improvement while also flagging a data regression; future PRs should inspect .sdata movement when adding extern constants or changing local static types.
naming_conventions: Retained address-based ftColl_* names such as ftColl_8007925C and ftColl_8007A06C because the PR did not provide semantic names.
Used established project macros/types such as Fighter_GObj, Item_GObj, HSD_GObj, GET_FIGHTER, HitCapsule, FighterHurtCapsule, ftCommonData, and PAD_STACK.
Named new enum value EntityKind_UNKNOWN in all caps for UNKNOWN, matching the diff exactly rather than normalizing it to EntityKind_Unknown.
Used field names that preserve current decomp uncertainty, such as x221B_b5, x189C_unk_num_frames, xDCE_flag.b5, shield_unk1, and unk_count.
Introduced local descriptive variables in implementations where the original symbols remain unknown, such as dmg_count, midpoint, coll_pos, coll_dist, best_entry, and scaled_dmg.
Header prototypes use concrete project types instead of UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS once known: Item*, Fighter*, HitCapsule*, Vec3*, HSD_GObj*, bool, u32, and float.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inserted PAD_STACK with specific byte counts, including 8, 16, 20, 24, 32, 46, and 0xB0, to control stack layout for matching.
Used #pragma dont_inline on/off and #pragma push/pop around functions such as ftColl_8007891C, ftColl_80078998, ftColl_8007925C, ftColl_8007BC90, and ftColl_8007BE3C.
Used explicit goto labels like retfalse, skip_hit_loop, catch_path, catch_elem_path, check_b3, next_item, and end to preserve original branch structure.
Disabled clang formatting around large structured functions where indentation and labels were likely shaped for matching rather than readability.
Replaced assert-report sequences with HSD_ASSERTREPORT calls using numeric constants such as 0xB7, 0xF9, 0x110, 0xE3, 0x888, 0x8C9, and 0x8DF.
Used casts to u32, s32, u8, int, and float around hit capsule and common-data math to influence codegen and preserve original arithmetic behavior.
Used MSL/trigf.h cosf/sinf in shield/item response code, suggesting explicit library selection mattered for emitted calls.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported matched code 62.55% (+0.04%, +1688 bytes) and matched data 38.10% (-0.00%, -8 bytes).
The bot reported 8 new full matches: ftColl_8007BC90, ftColl_800787B4, ftColl_80078538, ftColl_8007ABD0, ftColl_80078998, ftColl_8007891C, ftColl_8007AC9C, and ftColl_8007BBCC.

The bot reported 13 improvements in unmatched items, including ftColl_8007A06C to 87.21%, ftColl_8007925C to 97.54%, ftColl_80077C60 to 97.11%, ftColl_80077970 to 96.62%, ftColl_80077688 to 92.44%, ftColl_80079C70 to 96.54%, ftColl_8007B320 to 97.69%, ftColl_80079AB0 to 95.58%, ftColl_80079EA8 to 88.11%, ftColl_8007BAC0 to 98.96%, ftColl_8007B4E0 to 96.76%, ftColl_80076ED8 to 97.73%, and ftColl_8007BE3C to 90.57%.
The bot also reported one broken match: main/melee/ft/ftcoll .sdata dropped from 100.00% to 28.57%.
follow_up_queries: Investigate the .sdata regression reported by decomp.dev for main/melee/ft/ftcoll after this PR.
Search later PRs for semantic names or cleanup around ftColl_8007925C, ftColl_8007A06C, and ftColl_80077C60.
Check whether EntityKind_UNKNOWN was later renamed or expanded after this PR.
Look for later struct-layout refinements to ftData_x30, ftData_x38, ftDynamics.x8, Fighter.x1670, and DmgLogEntry.x20.
Compare subsequent matching tactics for the large item-vs-fighter collision scanner to see whether gotos/PAD_STACK pragmas were reduced after more symbols became known.
